title: Honor Thy Father

jokes.text: If we paid you by the mistake, you'd own this place by now.
jokes.setup: Louie berates Latka for sending someone out with a broken gas gauge
jokes.punchline: Suggests Latka makes so many mistakes he'd own the company if paid per mistake
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven insult that's specific to workplace dynamics. Well-structured with clear setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Okay, it's a deal.
jokes.setup: Louie's hypothetical insult about paying by the mistake
jokes.punchline: Latka completely misses the sarcasm and accepts it as a legitimate offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Latka's naivety. Simple but effective misdirection.

jokes.text: Louie calls for Ben Geretski multiple times with increasing frustration
jokes.setup: Latka wants to see the shop steward Ben Geretski
jokes.punchline: Louie repeatedly calls for someone who isn't there, building absurdity
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy setup but feels stretched. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: Louie reveals he IS the shift supervisor that Latka needs to report his grievance to
jokes.setup: Latka wants to file a grievance and needs to see the shift supervisor
jokes.punchline: The person he's complaining about is the person he has to complain to
jokes.explanation: Excellent structural irony. Classic workplace comedy setup with strong payoff showing the futility of bureaucracy.

jokes.text: You did the right thing reporting this, Latka. Thank you very much. Okay, now get your foreign butt back to work.
jokes.setup: Louie pretends to take Latka's grievance seriously as supervisor
jokes.punchline: Immediately dismisses it and continues the same behavior Latka complained about
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the grievance setup. Shows Louie's character - bureaucratically correct but personally unchanged.

jokes.text: Where did he grab you?
jokes.setup: Louie assumes Alex's sister is there to complain about harassment
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for salacious details
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Louie - shows his sleazy nature even when trying to be professional.

jokes.text: Oh!
jokes.setup: Latka speaks to Charlotte in his foreign language with romantic intentions
jokes.punchline: Charlotte's shocked reaction suggests Latka said something inappropriate or crude
jokes.explanation: Classic fish-out-of-water comedy - the cultured sister meets the crude foreign mechanic.

jokes.text: My guess is they dated them.
jokes.setup: Charlotte says Latka's family probably herded swine
jokes.punchline: Louie escalates to bestiality implication
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation joke that's perfectly in character for Louie's crude humor. Economical and memorable.

jokes.text: To me, New York is just a quiet rainy yesterday.
jokes.setup: Asked if she's a New Yorker
jokes.punchline: Gives an overly poetic, pretentious non-answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - shows Charlotte's pretentious, artsy personality through deliberately obtuse dialogue.

jokes.text: Starving something.
jokes.setup: Asked what she's raising funds for
jokes.punchline: Vague, disinterested response showing she doesn't really care
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - shows her superficial charity involvement with perfect economy. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I'd rather be the only woman on a Greek freighter.
jokes.setup: Louie offers his office to wait
jokes.punchline: Dramatically rejects with colorful metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven insult that shows both Charlotte's dramatic flair and disdain for Louie.

jokes.text: I wonder what 'uh, huh-huh, huh-huh' means.
jokes.setup: Latka had said something in his language earlier that shocked Charlotte
jokes.punchline: Tony innocently wonders about the meaning after Charlotte leaves
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Tony's innocent curiosity about what was obviously inappropriate.

jokes.text: So don't tell him till morning.
jokes.setup: Everyone agrees Alex will be in no shape to drive after hearing about his father
jokes.punchline: Louie's solution is to hide bad news for practical reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - prioritizes business over human decency with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: You're both from countries you have to take pills for.
jokes.setup: Latka and another foreign character are fighting
jokes.punchline: Louie's xenophobic but medically-framed insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent Louie line - creative xenophobia that's memorable and perfectly in character. Very quotable structure.

jokes.text: Okay, I understand, but we're even now.
jokes.setup: Louie offers to help Alex with his father problem
jokes.punchline: Immediately declares his debt paid when Alex declines
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie character moment - transactional view of friendship with immediate debt cancellation.

jokes.text: Yeah, it's a small country.
jokes.setup: Latka shares a saying that seems incomplete and anticlimactic
jokes.punchline: Explains it's complete because his country is small
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectation. Perfect structure and character-driven absurdism. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: She used to beat the heck out of me.
jokes.setup: Everyone wonders what Charlotte's childhood persuasion method was
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was simply violence
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the setup about Charlotte's persuasion method. Simple but effective revelation.

jokes.text: I'm glad you didn't make me guess.
jokes.setup: Charlotte mentions thinking of Scheherazade
jokes.punchline: Alex dryly acknowledges he wouldn't have guessed that reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex's practical nature contrasted with Charlotte's intellectualism.

jokes.text: I said he collected stamps. Cheap stamps.
jokes.setup: Charlotte had to write about her father's hobby when she was 11
jokes.punchline: 'Cheap stamps' as euphemism for cheap women
jokes.explanation: Excellent double entendre that works on multiple levels. Shows Charlotte's intelligence even as a child, with perfect wordplay structure.

jokes.text: Cheap, dirty stamps.
jokes.setup: The 'cheap stamps' punchline
jokes.punchline: Escalates with 'dirty' to complete the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the stamps joke, though slightly less impactful than the initial punchline.

jokes.text: You beg bad.
jokes.setup: Charlotte dramatically begs Alex to see their father
jokes.punchline: Alex critiques her begging technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alex response - deflects emotional moment with practical criticism. Very quotable structure.

jokes.text: I wish they had sent me to college. Then I'd have something to argue with.
jokes.setup: Charlotte has been making intellectual arguments
jokes.punchline: Admits her arguments aren't working because she lacks education
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlotte's self-awareness about her pretentiousness versus actual education.

jokes.text: Well, as a matter of fact... he was goosing me.
jokes.setup: Asked if Alex's father was overexerting himself when he had the heart attack
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was sexually harassing her
jokes.explanation: Strong misdirection that completely reframes the father's heart attack. Unexpected and memorable.

jokes.text: Talk about going with your boots on.
jokes.setup: Father had heart attack while sexually harassing someone
jokes.punchline: Twists the phrase 'dying with your boots on' for sexual context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that shows Charlotte's wit. Perfect economy and memorable phrasing with dark humor edge.

jokes.text: He must've realized the risks he was taking.
jokes.setup: Learning his father had a heart attack while harassing someone
jokes.punchline: Treats it like a calculated risk rather than showing concern
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex's detachment from his father through dark humor.

jokes.text: Tell him a few of the things that made him a good father.
jokes.setup: Alex asks what to say to his father
jokes.punchline: Charlotte suggests positive things despite all the negative stories they've shared
jokes.explanation: Ironic setup for the hospital scene where Alex will struggle to find positive things to say.

jokes.text: Yeah, I remember being really thirsty one night and you just marched right in there with a glass of water.
jokes.setup: Alex trying to think of good fatherly moments
jokes.punchline: Can only remember the most basic parental duty as if it were extraordinary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy showing how desperate Alex is to find positive memories. The mundane elevated to heroic is perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: It was fifth grade, Dad.
jokes.setup: Alex remembers his father asking what grade he was in
jokes.punchline: Finally gives the answer 30 years later
jokes.explanation: Incredibly poignant and funny moment. Perfect structure showing both the missed connection and Alex's emotional breakthrough. Deeply moving cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Well, he is now.
jokes.setup: Wrong patient asks if the man Alex talked to is a friend
jokes.punchline: Alex affirms the instant bond despite mistaken identity
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment that shows how the mistaken identity actually created a real connection. Good payoff to the whole sequence.

jokes.text: ¿Que pasa, que pasa, que pasa?
jokes.setup: Louie encounters some kind of commotion
jokes.punchline: Uses Spanish in his typical agitated way
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment. Shows Louie's personality but not particularly clever or memorable.

tmdb_overview: The cabbies are disturbed by Alex's nonchalance when confronted with the news of his father's heart attack.
insight: Deadpan restraint carries a slow-burn episode: 30 jokes in 24 minutes, minimal setup excess.
